text.title: Code Review Core Rules
text.summary: 5 hard rules every code-review run must follow.

rule.statement: PRs > 400 changed lines (excluding tests + lockfiles) are blocked from review until split.

rationale.source: SmartBear research on code review; Google Eng practices

rule.rationale: Review effectiveness collapses past ~400 lines; defects slip through.

rule.statement: Every comment carries one Conventional Comment label: praise / nitpick / suggestion / issue / question (+ optional decorations).
rule.rationale: Labels separate must-fix from preference; reviewer + author share vocabulary.

rule.statement: Review covers six categories: correctness, design, security, performance, tests, docs. A report missing any category MUST mark it 'not applicable'.
rule.rationale: Implicit categories let blind spots slip; explicit 'n/a' forces the reviewer to look.

rule.statement: Review emits ≤ 20 comments per PR; nits are batched into one 'nits' comment.

rationale.source: Internal review postmortems; OSS contribution guides

rule.rationale: Flooded PRs cause author burnout; the cap forces the reviewer to prioritise.

rule.statement: An LLM agent may draft the review but never be the sole approver; at least one human approval is required to merge.
rule.rationale: Approval is responsibility; agents cannot be held accountable.
